561.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'It was said: 'O Messenger of Allah, what about those who died praying toward Bayt al-Maqdis?' So Allah revealed: 'Allah would never let your faith go to waste.' (Al-Baqarah 2:143)'
561.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dikatakan: 'Wahai Rasulullah, bagaimana dengan orang-orang yang meninggal sementara mereka shalat menghadap Bayt al-Maqdis?' Maka Allah menurunkan: 'Allah tidak akan menyia-nyiakan iman kalian.' (Al-Baqarah 2:143)'

562.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to open the prayer with the takbir and open the recitation with 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabbil-alamin,' and he would conclude it with the taslim.
562.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membuka shalat dengan takbir dan membuka bacaan dengan 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabbil-alamin,' dan menutupnya dengan salam.

563.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ never stood for prayer without raising his hands fully extended.
563.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ tidak pernah berdiri untuk shalat tanpa mengangkat kedua tangannya secara terentang penuh.

564.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he began the prayer, he would say the takbir and then say: 'I have turned my face to the One who created the heavens and the earth, as a hanif, and I am not among the polytheists. Indeed my prayer and my sacrifice and my living and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the Worlds...'
564.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ali ibn Abi Talib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika memulai shalat, beliau mengucapkan takbir kemudian berdoa: 'Wajahtu wajhiya lilladzii fatharas-samaawaati wal-ardha haniifan wa maa ana minal-musyrikiin. Inna shalaatii wa nusukii wa mahyaaya wa mamaatii lillahi rabbil-'aalamiin...'

565.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Said al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he stood for the night prayer and said the takbir, he would say: 'Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, wa tabarakasmuka, wa ta'ala jadduka, wa la ilaha ghayruk. A'udhu billahis-sami'il-alim min al-shaytan al-rajim, min hamzihi wa nafthihi wa nafkhih,' then he would begin reading.
565.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Said al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika berdiri untuk shalat malam dan mengucapkan takbir, beliau berdoa: 'Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, wa tabarakasmuka, wa ta'ala jadduka, wa la ilaha ghayruk. A'udhu billahis-sami'il-alim minas-syaithanir-rajim, min hamzihi wa nafthihi wa nafkhih,' kemudian beliau mulai membaca.

566.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ, Abu Bakr, Umar, and Uthman used to open the recitation with 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabbil-alamin.'
566.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ, Abu Bakr, Umar, dan Utsman biasa membuka bacaan dengan 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabbil-alamin.'

567.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wail ibn Hujr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ place his right hand over his left near the wrist.
567.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Wail ibn Hujr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ meletakkan tangan kanannya di atas tangan kirinya dekat pergelangan tangan.

568.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubadah ibn al-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Whoever does not recite Umm al-Kitab (al-Fatiha) has no prayer.'
568.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubadah ibn al-Samit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang tidak membaca Umm al-Kitab (al-Fatihah), maka tidak ada shalat baginya.'

569.iwh_en: It was narrated from Samurah ibn Jundub (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to have two pauses of silence: one when he entered the prayer, and one when he finished the recitation. Imran ibn Husain denied this, so they wrote to Ubayy ibn Kab, who wrote back that Samurah was correct.
569.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Samurah ibn Jundub (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa memiliki dua jeda diam: satu ketika memasuki shalat, dan satu ketika selesai membaca. Imran ibn Husain mengingkarinya, maka mereka menulis kepada Ubay ibn Ka'b, yang membalas bahwa Samurah benar.

570.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to pause between the takbir and the recitation — he thought he said — a brief pause. So he asked him: 'O Messenger of Allah, may my father and mother be sacrificed for you, about your pause between the takbir and the recitation — what do you say?' He said: 'I say: O Allah, distance me from my sins as You have distanced the East from the West...'
570.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berdiam antara takbir dan bacaan — ia mengira beliau berkata — sejenak. Maka ia bertanya: 'Wahai Rasulullah, ayah dan ibuku sebagai tebusanmu, tentang diammu antara takbir dan bacaan — apa yang engkau baca?' Beliau menjawab: 'Aku membaca: Allahumma ba'id bayni wa bayna khatayaya...'

571.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When the reciter says: 'Not of those who have earned anger, nor of those who are astray,' (Al-Fatiha 1:7) and those behind him say Amin, and it coincides with the Amin of the inhabitants of the heavens, his previous sins will be forgiven.'
571.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila pembaca mengucapkan: 'Bukan jalan orang-orang yang dimurkai, dan bukan pula jalan orang-orang yang sesat,' (Al-Fatihah 1:7) dan orang-orang di belakangnya mengucapkan Amin, dan hal itu bertepatan dengan Amin para penghuni langit, maka dosa-dosanya yang telah lalu akan diampuni.'

572.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When the imam says: 'Not of those who have earned anger, nor of those who are astray,' say Amin, for indeed the angels say Amin, and the imam says Amin, and whoever's Amin coincides with the Amin of the angels, his previous sins will be forgiven.'
572.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila imam mengucapkan: 'Bukan jalan orang-orang yang dimurkai, dan bukan pula jalan orang-orang yang sesat,' ucapkanlah Amin, karena sesungguhnya para malaikat mengucapkan Amin, dan imam mengucapkan Amin, dan barangsiapa yang Aminnya bertepatan dengan Amin para malaikat, maka dosa-dosanya yang telah lalu diampuni.'

573.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wail ibn Hujr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he recited: 'Nor of those who are astray,' (Al-Fatiha 1:7) he would say: 'Amin,' raising his voice with it.
573.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Wail ibn Hujr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ, ketika membaca: 'Dan bukan pula jalan orang-orang yang sesat,' (Al-Fatihah 1:7) beliau mengucapkan: 'Amin,' dengan mengeraskan suaranya.

574.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that they prayed behind Abu Hurairah and when he bowed, he said the takbir; when he raised his head, he said: 'Sami'allahu liman hamidah, rabbana wa lakal-hamd.' Then he prostrated and said the takbir, and continued doing so, saying: 'I am the most similar of people to the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
574.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa mereka shalat di belakang Abu Hurairah dan ketika ia ruku', ia mengucapkan takbir; ketika mengangkat kepalanya, ia mengucapkan: 'Sami'allahu liman hamidah, rabbana wa lakal-hamd.' Kemudian ia sujud sambil mengucapkan takbir, dan terus melakukannya, seraya berkata: 'Akulah orang yang paling mirip shalatnya dengan Rasulullah ﷺ.'

575.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say the takbir at every raising and lowering, every standing and sitting.
575.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ mengucapkan takbir pada setiap pengangkatan dan penurunan, setiap berdiri dan duduk.

576.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he entered the prayer, he would say the takbir and raise his hands level with his shoulders; when he bowed he would do the same; when he raised his head from the ruku he would do the same; but he would not raise his hands between the two prostrations.
576.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika memasuki shalat, beliau mengucapkan takbir dan mengangkat kedua tangannya sejajar dengan bahunya; ketika ruku' beliau melakukan hal yang sama; ketika mengangkat kepalanya dari ruku' beliau melakukan hal yang sama; tetapi beliau tidak mengangkat tangannya di antara dua sujud.

577.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he said the takbir, he raised his hands until they were level with his ears; and similarly when he wanted to bow, and when he raised his head from the ruku.
577.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika mengucapkan takbir, beliau mengangkat kedua tangannya hingga sejajar dengan telinganya; begitu pula ketika hendak ruku', dan ketika mengangkat kepalanya dari ruku'.

578.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wail al-Hadrami that he prayed with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he would say the takbir when lowering and raising, raising his hands at the takbir, and giving the salam to the right and left.
578.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Wail al-Hadrami bahwa ia shalat bersama Rasulullah ﷺ, dan beliau mengucapkan takbir ketika turun dan naik, mengangkat tangannya saat takbir, dan memberi salam ke kanan dan ke kiri.

579.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (may Allah be pleased with him) that he came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ with a group of his people, young men, and they stayed with him for twenty nights. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was kind, and when he saw their longing for their families, he said: 'Return to your families, stay with them, teach them and instruct them. Pray as you have seen me pray.'
579.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ bersama sekelompok kaumnya, para pemuda, dan mereka tinggal bersamanya selama dua puluh malam. Rasulullah ﷺ bersikap lembut, dan ketika beliau melihat kerinduan mereka kepada keluarga mereka, beliau bersabda: 'Kembalilah kepada keluarga kalian, tinggallah bersama mereka, ajarkan dan bimbinglah mereka. Shalatlah sebagaimana kalian melihat aku shalat.'

580.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Said al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When three people gather, let one of them lead them, and the most deserving of the imamate is the one most proficient in Quran recitation.'
580.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Said al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila tiga orang berkumpul, hendaklah salah seorang dari mereka mengimami mereka, dan yang paling berhak menjadi imam adalah yang paling fasih bacaan Al-Qur'annya.'

581.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was staying with his maternal aunt Maimunah when the Prophet ﷺ came after Isha and prayed four rak'ahs, then slept, then stood up and prayed the witr.
581.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia sedang berada di rumah bibinya Maimunah ketika Nabi ﷺ datang setelah Isya dan shalat empat rakaat, kemudian tidur, kemudian berdiri dan shalat witir.

582.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ rode a horse and was thrown from it, so his right side was abraded. He led the prayer sitting, and we prayed sitting behind him. When he finished, he said: 'The imam is appointed to be followed, so when he prays standing, pray standing, and when he prays sitting, pray sitting.'
582.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menaiki kuda dan terjatuh darinya, sehingga sisi kanannya tergores. Beliau mengimami shalat sambil duduk, dan kami shalat sambil duduk di belakangnya. Ketika selesai, beliau bersabda: 'Imam itu dijadikan untuk diikuti, maka jika ia shalat berdiri, shalatlah berdiri, dan jika ia shalat duduk, shalatlah duduk.'

583.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked about the illness of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was gravely ill and he said: Have the people prayed? We said: No, O Messenger of Allah, they are waiting for you.' He said: 'Place water in the basin for me.' And he went out.'
583.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya tentang sakit Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ jatuh sakit parah dan beliau berkata: Apakah orang-orang telah shalat? Kami berkata: Belum, wahai Rasulullah, mereka menunggumu.' Beliau bersabda: 'Sediakan air di baskom untukku.' Dan beliau keluar.'

584.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sahl ibn Sad (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sit on the minbar, say the takbir, and the people said the takbir behind him. Then he bowed while on the minbar, then raised his head and stepped backwards to prostrate at the base of the minbar, then returned to the minbar.
584.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sahl ibn Sad (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ duduk di atas mimbar, mengucapkan takbir, dan orang-orang mengucapkan takbir di belakangnya. Kemudian beliau ruku' di atas mimbar, kemudian mengangkat kepalanya dan melangkah mundur untuk sujud di dasar mimbar, kemudian kembali ke mimbar.

585.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Masud al-Ansari that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, by Allah, I stay away from the Fajr prayer because of how long so-and-so makes it for us.' He said: he had never seen the Prophet ﷺ more angry in a sermon than he was that day.
585.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Masud al-Ansari bahwa seorang pria datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, demi Allah, aku tidak hadir shalat Subuh karena begitu panjangnya shalat yang dipimpin si Fulan untuk kami.' Ia berkata: Aku tidak pernah melihat Nabi ﷺ lebih marah dalam khutbahnya daripada hari itu.

586.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ had the lightest prayer of all people while making it complete.
586.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ memiliki shalat yang paling ringan di antara semua orang namun tetap sempurna.

587.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When the prayer is called, do not rise until you see me.'
587.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila shalat dikumandangkan, janganlah berdiri hingga kalian melihatku.'

588.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When the iqamah is made, do not rise until you see me.'
588.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila iqamah dikumandangkan, janganlah berdiri hingga kalian melihatku.'

589.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Straighten your rows, for straightening the rows is part of the perfection of the prayer.'
589.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Luruskanlah barisan-barisan kalian, karena meluruskan barisan adalah bagian dari kesempurnaan shalat.'

590.iwh_en: It was narrated from Al-Bara ibn Azib (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Straighten your rows lest your hearts differ.'
590.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Al-Bara ibn Azib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Luruskanlah barisan-barisan kalian agar hati kalian tidak berbeda.'

591.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Bara ibn Azib (may Allah be pleased with him) that he also said: 'Indeed Allah and His angels send blessings upon the first row or the first rows.'
591.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Bara ibn Azib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia juga berkata: 'Sesungguhnya Allah dan para malaikat-Nya bershalawat atas baris pertama atau baris-baris pertama.'

592.iwh_en: It was narrated from Irbad ibn Sariyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to seek forgiveness three times for the first row, and once for the second row.
592.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Irbad ibn Sariyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa memohon ampunan tiga kali untuk baris pertama, dan sekali untuk baris kedua.

593.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Masud al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to touch our shoulders during the prayer and say: 'Do not differ, lest your hearts differ. Let those of maturity and understanding stand nearest to me, then those next to them, then those next to them.'
593.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Masud al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa menyentuh bahu-bahu kami selama shalat dan bersabda: 'Janganlah berselisih, agar hati kalian tidak berselisih. Hendaklah yang dewasa dan berakal berdiri paling dekat denganku, kemudian yang setelah mereka, kemudian yang setelah mereka.'

594.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Let those of maturity and understanding stand nearest to me, then those next to them, then those next to them. Beware of the noise of the market place.'
594.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Hendaklah yang dewasa dan berakal berdiri paling dekat denganku, kemudian yang setelah mereka, kemudian yang setelah mereka. Jauhilah hiruk-pikuk pasar.'

595.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'The best rows for men are the first ones and the worst are the last ones. The best rows for women are the last ones and the worst are the first ones.'
595.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Baris terbaik bagi laki-laki adalah yang pertama dan terburuk adalah yang terakhir. Baris terbaik bagi perempuan adalah yang terakhir dan terburuk adalah yang pertama.'

596.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubayy ibn Kab (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ led the Fajr prayer, then turned to face them and said: 'Is so-and-so present?' They said: 'No.' He said: 'Is so-and-so present?' They said: 'No.' — referring to certain hypocrites who had not attended. He said: 'These two prayers are the heaviest on the hypocrites. If you knew what they contain, you would attend them even crawling.'
596.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubay ibn Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memimpin shalat Subuh, kemudian menghadap kepada mereka dan bersabda: 'Apakah si Fulan hadir?' Mereka berkata: 'Tidak.' Beliau bersabda: 'Apakah si Fulan hadir?' Mereka berkata: 'Tidak.' — mengacu pada beberapa orang munafik yang tidak hadir. Beliau bersabda: 'Dua shalat ini adalah yang paling berat bagi orang-orang munafik. Seandainya kalian mengetahui apa yang ada di dalamnya, niscaya kalian akan hadir walaupun dengan merangkak.'

597.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'There is no prayer heavier upon the hypocrites than Isha and Fajr. If they knew what reward is in them, they would come to them even if they had to crawl.'
597.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak ada shalat yang lebih berat bagi orang-orang munafik daripada shalat Isya dan Subuh. Seandainya mereka mengetahui pahala yang ada di dalamnya, niscaya mereka akan mendatanginya walaupun dengan merangkak.'

598.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'I have considered ordering my young men to gather firewood, then commanding a man to lead the people in prayer, then going myself to the people who stay away from this prayer and burning their houses over them. If one of them knew that he would find a meaty bone or two good morsels, he would attend Isha.'
598.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku telah mempertimbangkan untuk memerintahkan pemuda-pemudaku mengumpulkan kayu bakar, kemudian memerintahkan seseorang untuk mengimami shalat orang-orang, kemudian pergi sendiri kepada orang-orang yang tidak hadir pada shalat ini dan membakar rumah-rumah mereka. Seandainya salah seorang dari mereka mengetahui bahwa ia akan mendapat tulang berisi daging atau dua suapan makanan yang baik, niscaya ia akan hadir untuk shalat Isya.'

599.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that he stopped at Dajnan on a cold night and commanded a caller to announce: 'Pray in your homes.' Then he reported that the Prophet ﷺ used to command a caller to announce: 'Pray in your homes' when on a journey on a cold night or during rain.
599.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia singgah di Dajnan pada malam yang dingin dan memerintahkan seorang penyeru untuk mengumumkan: 'Shalatlah di tempat kalian.' Kemudian ia memberitahu bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa memerintahkan seorang penyeru untuk mengumumkan: 'Shalatlah di tempat kalian' ketika dalam perjalanan pada malam yang dingin atau saat hujan.

600.iwh_en: It was narrated from Said ibn al-Musayyib that he was asked: 'A man prays at home, then catches the imam while he is still praying — should he pray with him?' He said: 'Yes.' He was asked: 'Which one counts for him?' He said: 'The one he prays with the imam, for Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) told us that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When a man prays and then goes to the mosque and finds them praying, he should pray with them, and both prayers will count for him — one obligatory and one supererogatory."'
600.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Said ibn al-Musayyib bahwa ia ditanya: 'Seorang pria shalat di rumahnya, kemudian mendapati imam masih shalat — apakah ia shalat bersamanya?' Ia berkata: 'Ya.' Ditanya: 'Yang mana yang dihitung baginya?' Ia berkata: 'Yang ia shalat bersama imam, karena Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) menceritakan kepada kami bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Apabila seseorang shalat lalu pergi ke masjid dan mendapati mereka sedang shalat, hendaklah ia shalat bersama mereka, dan kedua shalatnya akan dihitung — satu wajib dan satu sunnah."'

601.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'A man's prayer in congregation is twenty-seven degrees more than his prayer alone.'
601.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalat seseorang berjamaah lebih utama dua puluh tujuh derajat dari shalatnya sendirian.'

602.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'If your wife asks your permission to go to the mosque, do not prevent her.'
602.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika istrimu meminta izinmu untuk pergi ke masjid, janganlah mencegahnya.'

603.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Do not prevent the female servants of Allah from the mosques of Allah, and let them go out without wearing perfume.'
603.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian mencegah hamba-hamba perempuan Allah dari masjid-masjid Allah, dan hendaklah mereka keluar tanpa memakai wewangian.'

604.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When dinner is served and the prayer is due, begin with dinner.'
604.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila makan malam dihidangkan dan waktu shalat tiba, mulailah dengan makan malam.'

605.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When dinner is served and the prayer is established, begin with dinner.'
605.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila makan malam dihidangkan dan iqamah shalat dikumandangkan, mulailah dengan makan malam.'

606.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When you come for the prayer, do not come running; come walking with tranquility. Whatever you catch, pray, and whatever you miss, complete.'
606.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila kalian datang untuk shalat, janganlah datang berlari; datanglah dengan berjalan tenang. Apa yang kalian dapati, shalatlah, dan apa yang kalian lewatkan, sempurnakanlah.'

607.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When you come to the prayer, maintain tranquility. Whatever you catch, pray, and whatever you miss, complete.'
607.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila kalian datang untuk shalat, bersikaplah tenang. Apa yang kalian dapati, shalatlah, dan apa yang kalian lewatkan, sempurnakanlah.'

608.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubayy ibn Kab (may Allah be pleased with him) that there was a man in Madinah whose house was farther from the mosque than anyone else who prayed facing the qiblah, and he used to pray every prayer with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Someone said to him: 'Why not buy a donkey to ride in the heat and darkness?' He said: 'By Allah, I would not like my house to be next to the mosque, for I want my walking to the mosque and my returning to be recorded for me.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Allah has combined all of that for you.'
608.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubay ibn Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ada seorang pria di Madinah yang rumahnya paling jauh dari masjid di antara semua orang yang shalat menghadap kiblat, dan ia selalu shalat setiap shalat bersama Rasulullah ﷺ. Seseorang berkata kepadanya: 'Mengapa tidak membeli keledai untuk ditunggangi di panas dan kegelapan?' Ia berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku tidak suka rumahku berada di sebelah masjid, karena aku ingin langkahku ke masjid dan kepulanganku dicatat untukku.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah telah mengumpulkan semua itu untukmu.'

609.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wabisah ibn Mabad (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man prayed behind the rows alone, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded him to repeat his prayer.
609.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Wabisah ibn Mabad (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang pria shalat di belakang barisan sendirian, maka Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkannya untuk mengulangi shalatnya.

610.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wabisah ibn Mabad that a man prayed behind the rows alone, and the Prophet ﷺ commanded him to repeat his prayer.
610.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Wabisah ibn Mabad bahwa seorang pria shalat di belakang barisan sendirian, dan Nabi ﷺ memerintahkannya untuk mengulangi shalatnya.
